title: buxus excisa subsp. costata
intro: Buxus excisa subsp. costata, also known as Boxwood, is a plant species within the Buxaceae family, characterized by its unique, fan-shaped or strap-like leaves. Traditionally, the leaves and bark of Buxus excisa subsp. costata have been used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing stress, improving sleep, and lowering blood pressure, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Buxus excisa subsp. costata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic use.
